title: chiogenes serpyllifolia
intro: Chiogenes serpyllifolia, also known as Sea Purslane or Sea Pinks, is a coastal succulent plant native to the northern hemisphere. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health conditions, offering benefits such as wound healing, reducing inflammation, and fighting off infections, making it a valuable remedy for skin issues and respiratory problems. Chiogenes serpyllifolia can be consumed as a herbal tea, used as a tincture, or applied topically as a salve or ointment.
